question: Were methods additional to database searching used to identify relevant reports?

terms MeSH and searching key words with the terms; (macrophage activation syndrome OR "Macrophage Activation Syndrome" [MeSH]) OR (Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis [MeSH] or hemophagocytic syndrome) AND (Kawasaki disease or Mucocutaneous lymph node syndrome) up to September 2016. A nonfilter for language and date was used in the search.

question: Were search strategy restrictions based on date, publication format, or language appropriate?
answers: A nonfilter for language and date was used in the search. Case reports and case series of MAS-KD were included with the available information,

question: Were efforts made to minimise error in selection of studies?
Were efforts made to minimise error in data collection?
Were sufficient study characteristics considered for both review authors and readers to be able to interpret the results?
answers:
TABLE 1. Demographic Features of the 69 Cases

Patients  Of the 69 cases analyzed, the mean age at diagnosis was 5.6 years (range, 7 weeks old to 17 years old), the majority of the cases were male 68% (47/69), and Asian
ethnicity was the most frequently reported (75%). A total of 77% of the patients presented complete KD (Table 1). Of the 69 analyzed cases, 34 reported the date of the diagnosis of KD and MAS, with the diagnosis of MAS made before the diagnosis of KD in 6% (2/34), with simultaneous presentation in 21% (7/34), and 73% (25/34) of patients with diagnosis of MAS after the diagnosis of KD.
